content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This refactors precompile storage to pass a lifetime-bound `StorageCtx` through generated contract and storage handlers instead of using scoped TLS or a no_std global pointer. It removes the `scoped-tls` dependency from the storage crate while keeping no-default-feature precompile consumers on the explicit context model.

fix(precompile-storage): use ordered no-std caches
fix(precompile-storage): enable no-std storage context
fix(precompile-storage): format feature list
fix(precompile-storage): tighten no-std storage context

refactor(precompile-storage): pass explicit storage context

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This refactor moves duplicated trace capture setup into the protocol test utilities and reuses it from derive tests. It generates the repeated rollup fork activation methods from a shared macro while preserving the public method names. It also adds a shared CLI entrypoint macro for binaries that parse Clap args, run the command, and exit on errors.
commit_subjects: refactor(common): share macro utilities
chore(ci): fix nightly rustfmt ordering

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This refactors the Base Azul action tests to share their common harness setup, transaction creation, and derivation assertions. The probe-specific CLZ, P256, and MODEXP tests now focus on their individual bytecode, calls, and storage checks while reusing a single test environment helper.
commit_subjects: refactor(consensus): share azul test harness

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This adds a `base update` subcommand that delegates verified binary updates to `baseup` in the same install directory as the running executable. It also updates `baseup` to install the unified `base` binary, verify signed release archives, and optionally check GitHub SLSA provenance. The release workflow now publishes the `base` artifact with checksums, GPG signatures, and attestations so the command has a signed artifact to install.
commit_subjects: feat(cli): add base update command
fix(cli): satisfy update docs clippy lint

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

This refactors batcher subscription wrappers to share generic stream, keepalive, and pending subscription implementations. The L2 block and L1 head subscription APIs remain unchanged while the duplicated WebSocket and no-op wrapper mechanics move into base-batcher-source.
commit_subjects: refactor(batcher): share subscription wrappers
fix(batcher): satisfy subscription clippy lint

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### Description
With https://github.com/base/base/pull/2742 and https://github.com/base/base/pull/2743 reducing the number of blocks that can be inserted concurrently. We should prefetch blocks from the source node to ensure we don't end up paying 5-10ms per block insert.

This PR adds prefetching of block data to follow mode.
commit_subjects: feat(follow): prefetch source L2 blocks

key_prs.author: danyalprout

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### Description
The engine api is optimization for serial insertion (with sparse trie cache etc). This PR waits for the previous insert to complete before continuing.
commit_subjects: chore(follow): wait for inserts to complete

key_prs.title: chore(follow): wait for inserts to complete

key_prs.author: danyalprout

content_signals.body_excerpt:
### Description
Reduce max ahead proofs default. With the migration to rocks db proof insertion is now quick, there's no real reason to allow the node to get 512 blocks (~20m ahead).

Allowing the node to be 512 blocks ahead also has issues if there's a failure, for example it takes ~1m to load/calc/insert a ~20m old block.
commit_subjects: chore(follow): reduce max ahead proofs default

key_prs.title: chore(follow): reduce max ahead proofs default

content_signals.body_excerpt:
op-supervisor is being deleted. These action tests drove it directly (via `Actors.Supervisor` — note that despite its name, `InteropDSL.ActSyncSupernode` drove the supervisor actor, not a supernode). Their protocol coverage now lives in op-supernode + op-acceptance-tests.

The emitter_contract tests were deleted because they don't provide enough value to justify having to keep op-supervisor around forever. The emitter contract is only used in tests so we'll notice if it stops working.

`interop_fork_test.go` is also deleted. Its coverage is reproduced by `op-acceptance-tests/tests/interop/upgrade/{pre,post}_test.go` (CrossL2Inbox pre/post-activation, cross-safe progression past activation, cross-chain message inclusion) and `tests/supernode/interop/activation*` (genesis activation + superroot_atTimestamp across the activation boundary).

After those deletions, only `super_root_script_test.go` still uses the interop action DSL. Everything in `op-e2e/actions/interop/dsl/` not reachable from it has been pruned:

- Deleted files: `assertions.go`, `emitter.go`, `inbox.go`, `dsl_user.go`, `transactions.go`, `outputs.go`, `super_root.go`, `utils.go`.
- Pruned exports: `ActSyncSupernode...
commit_subjects: chore(op-e2e): delete op-supervisor action tests
chore(op-e2e): goimports
chore(op-e2e): delete interop_fork_test.go
chore(op-e2e): prune dsl helpers unused after action-test deletions

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Replaces the EL-finalized-head cold-start heuristic with a deterministic verifiedDB-resume / SafeDB-first-entry model.

## Why

The previous startup logic was a tangle:
- `resolveFirstVerifiableTimestamp` used EL finalized head as the cold-start origin — the wrong signal (EL's view of finality, not proof that derivation has run).
- A retry loop conflated transient EL not-ready with permanent SafeDB gaps.
- Backfill ran before deciding the verification start timestamp.
- `firstVerifiableTimestamp` re-derived its value lazily, sometimes with I/O, from RPC handlers.

## What changed

- **Resume always wins.** Any committed `verifiedDB` entry resumes at `LastTimestamp+1` without consulting SafeDB or chain RPCs.
- **Cold start** waits for every chain to record a first SafeDB entry, then sets `verificationStartTimestamp = max(activationTimestamp, max first-safe-head timestamp)`. Wall-clock time is never consulted.
- **Backfill** clamps lower bound to \`max(activation, per-chain genesis, verificationStart - depth)\`. Hard fails if any chain can't serve the range. \`reconcileLogsDBTail\` runs only during cold-start backfill.
- **Start** splits into a fast init + stateful main loop. The ...
commit_subjects: feat(op-supernode): rework interop activity startup
fix(op-devstack/dsl): drop removed BackfillEndTimestamp accessor
refactor(op-supernode/interop): clarify startup loop and tolerate transient cold-start errors
docs(op-supernode/interop): refresh stale comments after startup rework
test(op-acceptance-tests): interop startup-rework resync tests (#20824)
fix(op-supernode/interop): drop unsynchronized waitingForSync read
test(op-supernode/interop): restore cold-start backfill edge-case coverage

key_prs.author: sebastianst

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Phase 4 of 6 — stacked on top of #20708 (Phase 3) → #20703 (Phase 2) → #20702 (Phase 1).

Replaces kona-node's `OnlinePipeline` + `DerivationActor::step` loop with `NodeDeriver`, an async driver around `kona_derive::Deriver` (Phase 3's pure, IO-free deriver). The new driver owns the L1 + L2 IO substrate, pulls L1 inputs on demand based on the L1 watcher's head height, fulfills `Derivation::NeedSpanBatchOverlap` by fetching the L2 range, and translates every `kona_derive::TraceEntry` to a structured `tracing::*` event — the *only* place the pure deriver's lifecycle events become logs.

The `Signal::Activation` site in the old async pipeline (`actor.rs:130`) collapses to a no-op: Phase 3's reset semantics establish that Holocene+ strict ordering subsumes the old soft-reset for hardfork boundaries, so no separate "activation" code path is needed downstream.

## What landed

- `crates/node/service/src/actors/derivation/deriver.rs` — new `NodeDeriver` wrapping the pure `Deriver`. Owns `AlloyChainProvider`, `AlloyL2ChainProvider`, `OnlineBlobProvider`. Matches on `Derivation` per step: `NeedL1Input` → `fetch_l1_block_data` + `extract_l1_input` + `add_l1_input`; `NeedSpanBa...
commit_subjects: feat(kona/derive): pure sync deriver with full span-batch overlap check
feat(kona/node): migrate to NodeDeriver wrapping pure::Deriver (phase 4/6)

key_prs.title: feat(kona/node): migrate to NodeDeriver wrapping pure::Deriver (phase 4/6)

key_prs.author: ajsutton

content_signals.body_excerpt:
SuperSystem now boots a single op-supernode that owns one virtual op-node per L2 chain, instead of running a standalone op-supervisor alongside separate op-nodes. Per-chain CL RPC is reached at \`<supernode>/<chainID>\`; the batcher's \`RollupRpc\` and the dispute helpers use that routed URL.

\`op-e2e/interop/interop_test.go\` is deleted — it only exercised op-supervisor RPCs (\`FinalizedL1\`, \`CheckAccessList\`, \`AddL2RPC\`) that have no supernode counterpart and are now covered by op-supernode and op-acceptance-tests.

Action tests under \`op-e2e/actions/interop/\` are intentionally untouched and continue to use op-supervisor.

Towards #20814.

## Test plan
- [x] \`TestCreateSuperCannonGame/mt-cannon\` passes locally end-to-end against the new SuperSystem.
- [ ] Full \`super_test.go\` run in CI.

refactor(op-e2e): move supernode sync wait into startSuperCannonGameOfType
docs(op-e2e): drop historical comparisons from supersystem_l2 comments
docs(op-e2e): restore unchanged comments in supersystem.go
review: drop unused NewRollupConfigsFromParsed calls; preserve original ctx for waitForSupernodePastL1

key_prs.title: chore(op-e2e): replace op-supervisor with op-supernode in super_test path

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds two acceptance tests for the cold-start resync path introduced by ethereum-optimism/optimism#20823 — post-activation and pre-activation variants. Each stops the supernode, deletes its full on-disk data dir, starts a fresh instance against the same chains/virtual nodes, and asserts the verifier resumes and cross-safe advances on both chains.

Replaces the old `RestartInteropActivity(wipeLogsDBs bool)` test primitive (wiping only logs DBs is no longer meaningful under the rework) with `RestartWithFreshDataDir` — a full supernode Stop + data-dir wipe + Start. The sysgo `SuperNode` picks up a long-lived `tcpproxy` so its RPC URL survives the restart. The old `TestSupernodeLogBackfill_HappyPath` is deleted; its scenario is unreachable under the rework and is superseded by these tests.

Tests 2 & 4 from the spec at `plans/interop-startup-rework-acceptance-tests.md` (EL-data-wipe variants) need a dual-EL preset and will land in a follow-up PR.
commit_subjects: test(op-acceptance-tests): add interop startup-rework resync tests
review: trim docs, drop local testutil, reuse existing DSL primitives
test: drop activation delay from post-activation resync test
test: simplify resync tests to reuse existing DSL primitives
test: collapse startup-resync tests into a single package
test: assert backfill coverage in post-activation resync

key_prs.author: ajsutton

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds tests 2 and 4 from the interop startup-rework acceptance plan, folded into the post- and pre-activation tests as `EL data wiped` subtests via `t.Run`. The fix the EL-wiped path was originally written to exercise has been split out to #20855; these tests cover the resync mechanics on their own.

`TwoL2SupernodeInteropPeerEL` extends `TwoL2SupernodeInterop` the same way `TwoL2SupernodeFollowL2` does. The supernode VN runs in `Sync.SyncMode = ELSync`, reqresp + discovery disabled; block content reaches the supernode-fronted EL over execution-layer devp2p from a sibling sequencer EL, CL pubsub only carries head announcements.

Peer tracking moved from DSL into sysgo: `OpReth`, `OpGeth`, and `SuperNode` embed a `peerRegistry`, `connectL2ELPeersBidi`/`connectL2CLPeers` register a re-dial closure on both participants, and each node replays its registry as the final step of `Start`/`startLocked`. Restart-after-wipe now uses the *same* dial code as initial setup (bidi + trusted for ELs), instead of the previous one-way+untrusted DSL replay.

op-reth state wipe: new `stack.OnDiskStateWiper` interface, implemented on `*OpReth` (`RemoveAll` data + proof-history dirs, re-run `op-reth in...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
**Description**

This PR adds `BackfillJob::run_with_snapshot` which reads trie state from the snapshot during backfill (instead of the V2 history merge-walk) and atomically advances the snapshot anchor with each prepended block.

Behavior:
- **No snapshot present?** `run_with_snapshot` bootstraps one at the current `earliest` via `SnapshotInitJob` and proceeds.
- **`Ready` snapshot at `earliest`?** Proceeds, using snapshot cursors for both diff computation and state-root validation.
- **Snapshot at a different anchor?** Errors with `SnapshotAnchorMismatch` so callers explicitly drop / re-init.
- Per-block: one rw-tx does (1) `update_snapshot` (2) `prepend_block` (3) validate state root at `E-1` against reth's header (4) commit — keeping the snapshot and the proofs window in lockstep.

**Tests**

- [x] snapshot based backfill test added

**Additional context**

NA

**Metadata**

Closes #20792
commit_subjects: feat(op-reth/proofs): integrate snapshot into backfill
clippy fixes

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- expose initial EL-sync state from op-node through supernode virtual nodes and chain containers
- gate interop cold-start before reading first SafeDB history while any VN is still initial EL syncing
- add a cold-start regression test that verifies SafeDB is not queried during initial EL sync

## Testing
- `mise exec -- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop ./op-supernode/supernode/chain_container ./op-supernode/supernode/chain_container/virtual_node ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/supernode ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/superroot ./op-node/node -count=1`

Stacked on #20823.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Phase 4 of 6 — stacked on top of #20708 (Phase 3) → #20703 (Phase 2) → #20702 (Phase 1).

Replaces kona-node's `OnlinePipeline` + `DerivationActor::step` loop with `NodeDeriver`, an async driver around `kona_derive::Deriver` (Phase 3's pure, IO-free deriver). The new driver owns the L1 + L2 IO substrate, pulls L1 inputs on demand based on the L1 watcher's head height, fulfills `Derivation::NeedSpanBatchOverlap` by fetching the L2 range, and translates every `kona_derive::TraceEntry` to a structured `tracing::*` event — the *only* place the pure deriver's lifecycle events become logs.

The `Signal::Activation` site in the old async pipeline (`actor.rs:130`) collapses to a no-op: Phase 3's reset semantics establish that Holocene+ strict ordering subsumes the old soft-reset for hardfork boundaries, so no separate "activation" code path is needed downstream.

## What landed

- `crates/node/service/src/actors/derivation/deriver.rs` — new `NodeDeriver` wrapping the pure `Deriver`. Owns `AlloyChainProvider`, `AlloyL2ChainProvider`, `OnlineBlobProvider`. Matches on `Derivation` per step: `NeedL1Input` → `fetch_l1_block_data` + `extract_l1_input` + `add_l1_input`; `NeedSpanBa...
commit_subjects: feat(kona/derive): pure sync deriver with full span-batch overlap check
feat(kona/node): migrate to NodeDeriver wrapping pure::Deriver (phase 4/6)

draft.title: feat(kona/node): migrate to NodeDeriver wrapping pure::Deriver (phase 4/6)

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- replace the cold-start EL-finalized backfill anchor with a latched newest-local-safe handoff
- require the handoff timestamp to be available through OptimisticAt before sealing backfill logs
- make historical superroot queries below local verifier coverage pessimistic instead of composing optimistic data
- halt startup backfill immediately on permanent SafeDB history gaps

## Validation
- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/superroot -count=1
- mise exec -- env JUST_UNSTABLE=1 just build-contracts

Note: contract build completed with existing Solidity warnings around payable fallback without receive.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
op-supervisor is being deleted. These action tests drove it directly (via `Actors.Supervisor` — note that despite its name, `InteropDSL.ActSyncSupernode` drove the supervisor actor, not a supernode). Their protocol coverage now lives in op-supernode + op-acceptance-tests.

The emitter_contract tests were deleted because they don't provide enough value to justify having to keep op-supervisor around forever. The emitter contract is only used in tests so we'll notice if it stops working.

`interop_fork_test.go` is also deleted. Its coverage is reproduced by `op-acceptance-tests/tests/interop/upgrade/{pre,post}_test.go` (CrossL2Inbox pre/post-activation, cross-safe progression past activation, cross-chain message inclusion) and `tests/supernode/interop/activation*` (genesis activation + superroot_atTimestamp across the activation boundary).

After those deletions, only `super_root_script_test.go` still uses the interop action DSL. Everything in `op-e2e/actions/interop/dsl/` not reachable from it has been pruned:

- Deleted files: `assertions.go`, `emitter.go`, `inbox.go`, `dsl_user.go`, `transactions.go`, `outputs.go`, `super_root.go`, `utils.go`.
- Pruned exports: `ActSyncSupernode...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Replaces the EL-finalized-head cold-start heuristic with a deterministic verifiedDB-resume / SafeDB-first-entry model.

## Why

The previous startup logic was a tangle:
- `resolveFirstVerifiableTimestamp` used EL finalized head as the cold-start origin — the wrong signal (EL's view of finality, not proof that derivation has run).
- A retry loop conflated transient EL not-ready with permanent SafeDB gaps.
- Backfill ran before deciding the verification start timestamp.
- `firstVerifiableTimestamp` re-derived its value lazily, sometimes with I/O, from RPC handlers.

## What changed

- **Resume always wins.** Any committed `verifiedDB` entry resumes at `LastTimestamp+1` without consulting SafeDB or chain RPCs.
- **Cold start** waits for every chain to record a first SafeDB entry, then sets `verificationStartTimestamp = max(activationTimestamp, max first-safe-head timestamp)`. Wall-clock time is never consulted.
- **Backfill** clamps lower bound to \`max(activation, per-chain genesis, verificationStart - depth)\`. Hard fails if any chain can't serve the range. \`reconcileLogsDBTail\` runs only during cold-start backfill.
- **Start** splits into a fast init + stateful main loop. The ...
commit_subjects: feat(op-supernode): rework interop activity startup
fix(op-devstack/dsl): drop removed BackfillEndTimestamp accessor
refactor(op-supernode/interop): clarify startup loop and tolerate transient cold-start errors
docs(op-supernode/interop): refresh stale comments after startup rework
test(op-acceptance-tests): interop startup-rework resync tests (#20824)
fix(op-supernode/interop): drop unsynchronized waitingForSync read
test(op-supernode/interop): restore cold-start backfill edge-case coverage

content_signals.body_excerpt:
SuperSystem now boots a single op-supernode that owns one virtual op-node per L2 chain, instead of running a standalone op-supervisor alongside separate op-nodes. Per-chain CL RPC is reached at \`<supernode>/<chainID>\`; the batcher's \`RollupRpc\` and the dispute helpers use that routed URL.

\`op-e2e/interop/interop_test.go\` is deleted — it only exercised op-supervisor RPCs (\`FinalizedL1\`, \`CheckAccessList\`, \`AddL2RPC\`) that have no supernode counterpart and are now covered by op-supernode and op-acceptance-tests.

Action tests under \`op-e2e/actions/interop/\` are intentionally untouched and continue to use op-supervisor.

Towards #20814.

## Test plan
- [x] \`TestCreateSuperCannonGame/mt-cannon\` passes locally end-to-end against the new SuperSystem.
- [ ] Full \`super_test.go\` run in CI.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds two acceptance tests for the cold-start resync path introduced by ethereum-optimism/optimism#20823 — post-activation and pre-activation variants. Each stops the supernode, deletes its full on-disk data dir, starts a fresh instance against the same chains/virtual nodes, and asserts the verifier resumes and cross-safe advances on both chains.

Replaces the old `RestartInteropActivity(wipeLogsDBs bool)` test primitive (wiping only logs DBs is no longer meaningful under the rework) with `RestartWithFreshDataDir` — a full supernode Stop + data-dir wipe + Start. The sysgo `SuperNode` picks up a long-lived `tcpproxy` so its RPC URL survives the restart. The old `TestSupernodeLogBackfill_HappyPath` is deleted; its scenario is unreachable under the rework and is superseded by these tests.

Tests 2 & 4 from the spec at `plans/interop-startup-rework-acceptance-tests.md` (EL-data-wipe variants) need a dual-EL preset and will land in a follow-up PR.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds tests 2 and 4 from the interop startup-rework acceptance plan, folded into the post- and pre-activation tests as `EL data wiped` subtests via `t.Run`. The fix the EL-wiped path was originally written to exercise has been split out to #20855; these tests cover the resync mechanics on their own.

`TwoL2SupernodeInteropPeerEL` extends `TwoL2SupernodeInterop` the same way `TwoL2SupernodeFollowL2` does. The supernode VN runs in `Sync.SyncMode = ELSync`, reqresp + discovery disabled; block content reaches the supernode-fronted EL over execution-layer devp2p from a sibling sequencer EL, CL pubsub only carries head announcements.

Peer tracking moved from DSL into sysgo: `OpReth`, `OpGeth`, and `SuperNode` embed a `peerRegistry`, `connectL2ELPeersBidi`/`connectL2CLPeers` register a re-dial closure on both participants, and each node replays its registry as the final step of `Start`/`startLocked`. Restart-after-wipe now uses the *same* dial code as initial setup (bidi + trusted for ELs), instead of the previous one-way+untrusted DSL replay.

op-reth state wipe: new `stack.OnDiskStateWiper` interface, implemented on `*OpReth` (`RemoveAll` data + proof-history dirs, re-run `op-reth in...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Builds on #20635. Splits the single `kona-prestates` melange package into four composable packages so each piece uses stock melange `uses:` pipelines and decouples toolchain from app code:

| Package | Pipeline | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rust-nightly-2026-02-20` | `uses: fetch` | Hash-pinned nightly toolchain + `rust-src` from `static.rust-lang.org` |
| `cannon` | `uses: go/build` (x2) | MIPS64 FPVM emulator (`cannon64-impl` embedded into `multicannon`) |
| `kona-client-elf` | `uses: cargo/build-no-auditable` (x2) | `kona-client` + `kona-client-int` MIPS64 ELFs |
| `kona-prestates` | composed | Runs cannon over installed ELFs to emit `prestate.bin.gz` + `prestate-proof.json` |

`apko` composes the final image from `kona-prestates` alone.

### Why split

- Touch only Go side → `cannon` rebuilds, `kona-client-elf` APK reused.
- Touch only Rust side → `kona-client-elf` rebuilds, `cannon` APK reused.
- Nightly toolchain provenance lives in its own auditable package; app builds depend on it as a normal Wolfi-style APK rather than calling `rustup` inline.

### cargo/build-no-auditable

Local pipeline override at `melange-pipelines/cargo/build-no-auditable.yaml`. Id...
commit_subjects: feat(kona): compose reproducible prestates from per-component melange packages

open.title: feat(kona): compose prestates from per-component melange packages

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Adds [`notices/interop-prep.mdx`](docs/public-docs/notices/interop-prep.mdx) — the node-operator action checklist for the upcoming OP Sepolia + Unichain Sepolia interop activation (targeted for late June 2026).

The notice walks operators through:

1. **Standing up `op-reth` execution clients per chain** (`--chain=optimism-sepolia` and `--chain=unichain-sepolia`).
2. **Standing up `op-supernode`** covering both chains, with shared L1 + beacon and per-chain virtual-node configuration.
3. **Migrating every `op-node` in the fleet to Light CL mode** via `--l2.follow.source` pointed at the supernode's per-chain RPC prefix.
4. **Placing supernodes behind a consensus-aware `proxyd`** (HA pool of at least three instances).

Also updates the existing topology and supernode pages to reflect the new reality:

- `notices/specialized-node-topology.mdx` — top Warning reframed as a concrete requirement for OP Sepolia / Unichain Sepolia at activation; descriptions throughout reflect that the source tier can be `op-node` (non-interop chains) or `op-supernode` (interop chains).
- `op-stack/interop/supernode.mdx` and `node-operators/guides/configuration/supernode.mdx` — added forward-l...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
Adds two acceptance tests for the cold-start resync path introduced by ethereum-optimism/optimism#20823 — post-activation and pre-activation variants. Each stops the supernode, deletes its full on-disk data dir, starts a fresh instance against the same chains/virtual nodes, and asserts the verifier resumes and cross-safe advances on both chains.

Replaces the old `RestartInteropActivity(wipeLogsDBs bool)` test primitive (wiping only logs DBs is no longer meaningful under the rework) with `RestartWithFreshDataDir` — a full supernode Stop + data-dir wipe + Start. The sysgo `SuperNode` picks up a long-lived `tcpproxy` so its RPC URL survives the restart. The old `TestSupernodeLogBackfill_HappyPath` is deleted; its scenario is unreachable under the rework and is superseded by these tests.

Tests 2 & 4 from the spec at `plans/interop-startup-rework-acceptance-tests.md` (EL-data-wipe variants) need a dual-EL preset and will land in a follow-up PR.

test: assert backfill coverage in post-activation resync

content_signals.body_excerpt:
op-supervisor is being deleted. These action tests drove it directly (via `Actors.Supervisor` — note that despite its name, `InteropDSL.ActSyncSupernode` drove the supervisor actor, not a supernode). Their protocol coverage now lives in op-supernode + op-acceptance-tests.

The emitter_contract tests were deleted because they don't provide enough value to justify having to keep op-supervisor around forever. The emitter contract is only used in tests so we'll notice if it stops working.

`interop_fork_test.go` is also deleted. Its coverage is reproduced by `op-acceptance-tests/tests/interop/upgrade/{pre,post}_test.go` (CrossL2Inbox pre/post-activation, cross-safe progression past activation, cross-chain message inclusion) and `tests/supernode/interop/activation*` (genesis activation + superroot_atTimestamp across the activation boundary).

After those deletions, only `super_root_script_test.go` still uses the interop action DSL. Everything in `op-e2e/actions/interop/dsl/` not reachable from it has been pruned:

- Deleted files: `assertions.go`, `emitter.go`, `inbox.go`, `dsl_user.go`, `transactions.go`, `outputs.go`, `super_root.go`, `utils.go`.
- Pruned exports: `ActSyncSupernode...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Adds [`notices/interop-prep.mdx`](docs/public-docs/notices/interop-prep.mdx) — the node-operator action checklist for the upcoming OP Sepolia + Unichain Sepolia interop activation (targeted for late June 2026).

The notice walks operators through:

1. **Standing up `op-reth` execution clients per chain** (`--chain=optimism-sepolia` and `--chain=unichain-sepolia`).
2. **Standing up `op-supernode`** covering both chains, with shared L1 + beacon and per-chain virtual-node configuration.
3. **Migrating every `op-node` in the fleet to Light CL mode** via `--l2.follow.source` pointed at the supernode's per-chain RPC prefix.
4. **Placing supernodes behind a consensus-aware `proxyd`** (HA pool of at least three instances).

Also updates the existing topology and supernode pages to reflect the new reality:

- `notices/specialized-node-topology.mdx` — top Warning reframed as a concrete requirement for OP Sepolia / Unichain Sepolia at activation; descriptions throughout reflect that the source tier can be `op-node` (non-interop chains) or `op-supernode` (interop chains).
- `op-stack/interop/supernode.mdx` and `node-operators/guides/configuration/supernode.mdx` — added forward-l...

Stacked on #20823.

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Builds on #20635. Splits the single `kona-prestates` melange package into four composable packages so each piece uses stock melange `uses:` pipelines and decouples toolchain from app code:

| Package | Pipeline | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `rust-nightly-2026-02-20` | `uses: fetch` | Hash-pinned nightly toolchain + `rust-src` from `static.rust-lang.org` |
| `cannon` | `uses: go/build` (x2) | MIPS64 FPVM emulator (`cannon64-impl` embedded into `multicannon`) |
| `kona-client-elf` | `uses: cargo/build-no-auditable` (x2) | `kona-client` + `kona-client-int` MIPS64 ELFs |
| `kona-prestates` | composed | Runs cannon over installed ELFs to emit `prestate.bin.gz` + `prestate-proof.json` |

`apko` composes the final image from `kona-prestates` alone.

### Why split

- Touch only Go side → `cannon` rebuilds, `kona-client-elf` APK reused.
- Touch only Rust side → `kona-client-elf` rebuilds, `cannon` APK reused.
- Nightly toolchain provenance lives in its own auditable package; app builds depend on it as a normal Wolfi-style APK rather than calling `rustup` inline.

### cargo/build-no-auditable

Local pipeline override at `melange-pipelines/cargo/build-no-auditable.yaml`. Id...

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary

Phase 4 of 6 — stacked on top of #20708 (Phase 3) → #20703 (Phase 2) → #20702 (Phase 1).

Replaces kona-node's `OnlinePipeline` + `DerivationActor::step` loop with `NodeDeriver`, an async driver around `kona_derive::Deriver` (Phase 3's pure, IO-free deriver). The new driver owns the L1 + L2 IO substrate, pulls L1 inputs on demand based on the L1 watcher's head height, fulfills `Derivation::NeedSpanBatchOverlap` by fetching the L2 range, and translates every `kona_derive::TraceEntry` to a structured `tracing::*` event — the *only* place the pure deriver's lifecycle events become logs.

The `Signal::Activation` site in the old async pipeline (`actor.rs:130`) collapses to a no-op: Phase 3's reset semantics establish that Holocene+ strict ordering subsumes the old soft-reset for hardfork boundaries, so no separate "activation" code path is needed downstream.

## What landed

- `crates/node/service/src/actors/derivation/deriver.rs` — new `NodeDeriver` wrapping the pure `Deriver`. Owns `AlloyChainProvider`, `AlloyL2ChainProvider`, `OnlineBlobProvider`. Matches on `Derivation` per step: `NeedL1Input` → `fetch_l1_block_data` + `extract_l1_input` + `add_l1_input`; `NeedSpanBa...
commit_subjects: feat(kona/derive): pure sync deriver with full span-batch overlap check
feat(kona/node): migrate to NodeDeriver wrapping pure::Deriver (phase 4/6)

content_signals.body_excerpt:
## Summary
- replace the cold-start EL-finalized backfill anchor with a latched newest-local-safe handoff
- require the handoff timestamp to be available through OptimisticAt before sealing backfill logs
- make historical superroot queries below local verifier coverage pessimistic instead of composing optimistic data
- halt startup backfill immediately on permanent SafeDB history gaps

## Validation
- go test ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/interop ./op-supernode/supernode/activity/superroot -count=1
- mise exec -- env JUST_UNSTABLE=1 just build-contracts

Note: contract build completed with existing Solidity warnings around payable fallback without receive.

anchor supernode backfill to safedb start
preserve historical optimistic superroots
clarify interop startup backfill flow
clean up interop startup backfill flow
